events.title: Iran Claims Strikes on Bushehr and Konarak, US Denies Involvement
events.desc: Iranian state media reported that military headquarters in Bushehr province and the city of Konarak were struck. The United States has denied carrying out these attacks, escalating tensions between the two nations.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Traffic Plummets Amid Renewed US-Iran Hostilities
events.desc: Traffic through the vital Strait of Hormuz has experienced a significant decline following renewed strikes between the United States and Iran. Brent crude prices remained stable despite the disruption to this critical maritime waterway.

events.title: US and Iran Trade Strikes; Strait of Hormuz Traffic Halts
events.desc: The United States and Iran have engaged in a new cycle of strikes, with traffic through the Strait of Hormuz grinding to a halt. Analysts suggest that differing interpretations of a memorandum of understanding related to Hormuz are at the heart of the escalation.

events.title: US Military Reports Strikes on Iranian Missile Sites and Command Centers
events.desc: The US military announced it had conducted strikes targeting Iranian missile sites and command centers. Iran responded by claiming to have attacked US bases, indicating a significant escalation in kinetic exchanges.

events.title: Trump Threatens Further Strikes on Iran Amid Tehran's Warning of Retaliation
events.desc: During a NATO summit, US President Trump indicated that the United States would likely conduct further strikes against Iran. This statement came as Tehran issued a warning of a 'fearless' response to any escalatory actions.

events.title: Iran Mourns Supreme Leader; Calls for Revenge
events.desc: Iranians gathered in Mashhad for the burial of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, with many expressing calls for revenge and rejecting any compromise with the West. The funeral ceremonies were marked by strong displays of loyalty to the Islamic Republic.

events.title: US and Iran Ceasefire Fraying Amid Tit-for-Tat Strikes
events.desc: The memorandum of understanding between the United States and Iran, signed three weeks ago, appears to be rapidly deteriorating. Despite earlier skirmishes, the agreement faced mounting pressure due to mutual claims of violations and bad faith, increasing the threat of full-scale hostilities.

events.title: Damascus Bombings Linked to Islamic State in Preliminary Probe
events.desc: Syrian authorities stated that preliminary investigations into the cell responsible for recent bombings in Damascus suggest an affiliation with the Islamic State. One member of the cell was tracked, leading to the identification of the rest of the group.

events.title: Iran Nuclear and Military Damage Revealed in Released Satellite Images
events.desc: Restricted satellite imagery has revealed damage to Iranian targets, including ballistic missile infrastructure, nuclear sites, and naval bases, following recent exchanges.

events.title: US Lawmakers Urge Debate on Israel Plans Amid Military Spending Debate
events.desc: A group of U.S. lawmakers is pressing for a debate on proposals to deepen ties with Israel before approving military spending legislation, signaling growing dissent within the Democratic party regarding support for the Netanyahu government.

events.title: Headteacher Among Four Killed in Israeli Attack on Lebanon
events.desc: Lebanese state media reported that an Israeli strike killed four individuals, including a headteacher, who were travelling in a car. This marks one of the deadliest attacks on Lebanon since the latest ceasefire.

events.title: Houthi Threat to Maritime Shipping Re-examined Amid Red Sea Tensions
events.desc: An updated analysis of the Houthi threat to maritime shipping highlights that the international response in the Red Sea has been insufficient. The discussion revisits arguments for a UN-led solution amidst ongoing global attention to maritime security.

events.title: Somali Pirates Re-emerge Amid Dormant Threat
events.desc: Recent pirate attacks off the Horn of Africa, including the hijacking of three merchant vessels, signal a resurgence of Somali piracy. The threat had become dormant due to sustained coalition efforts, but is now re-emerging amidst changing security dynamics.

events.title: Gaza Aid Worker Killed by Israel
events.desc: An Israeli strike in Gaza resulted in the death of aid worker Mohammed al-Wahidi, along with three others. The incident occurred on the eve of a major World Cup match, highlighting the ongoing impact of the conflict on civilians.
